text_blocks.text: Royal assent: After the bill has been
passed, the Presiding Officer submits it to the
Monarch for royal assent and it becomes an
Act of the Scottish Parliament. However he
cannot do so until a 4-week period has elapsed,
during which the Law Officers of the Scottish
Government or UK Government can refer the bill
to the Supreme Court of the United Kingdom
for a ruling on whether it is within the powers
of the Parliament. Acts of the Scottish
Parliament do not begin with a conventional
enacting formula. Instead they begin with a
phrase that reads: "The Bill for this Act of the
Scottish Parliament was passed by the
